steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The subject is the gerund phrase 'Having proportionally large populations.' A gerund phrase, no matter how long, functions as a single singular noun and requires a singular verb.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'is': singular present tense — agrees with the singular gerund-phrase subject. ✓
  • Choice B — 'were': plural past tense — wrong number and wrong tense. ✗
  • Choice C — 'are': plural present tense — violates singular subject agreement. ✗
  • Choice D — 'have been': plural present perfect — violates singular subject agreement. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
